_doc: BWM Client Locks — machine-checkable contract for asap-pest-wildlife. Each assertion is a perceptual failure mode the team committed to never recur. Loaded by Brain's tools/bwm-client-locks-qa.py during PATCH and FULL BUILD. Drift from these locks = build failure. Source incidents: John's 2026-05-08 website-review email + Nehemiah's 2026-05-11 meeting (transcript at ~/Downloads/nehemiah-2026-05-11-transcript.md) + John's 2026-05-15 v8.1 review (over-removed ampersand + phone-tooth overlap).

required_strings.rationale: Sealing entry points is core to wildlife process — was replaced by 'Empathy' in v6, restored 2026-05-12 per client review.
Step 1 of canonical 5-step process.
Step 2 of canonical 5-step process.
Step 4 of canonical 5-step process.
Step 5 of canonical 5-step process.

Allaine 2026-05-18 flag: nav transparent overlapped content on scroll. Header MUST carry id="site-header" so main.js can toggle .is-scrolled (CSS in style.css adds bg-cream/95 + backdrop-blur). Removing the id silently regresses nav readability on scroll.
Same — applies sitewide.
Same — applies sitewide.

Robert 2026-05-18 flag (v8.4): the More tab's ampersand must sit BEFORE 'More' with negative margin-right (underlaps the M from the left), matching removeasap.com reference. The we-remove-amp class on the img anchors the structure; removing it regresses to the spaced-after layout that prompted the flag.

John Mukasa 2026-03-09 ask (v8.4 Phase 1 close-out): bee page must surface the HBR (Honey Bee Removal Operator) certification under the Georgia Structural Pest Control Act. This is ASAP's primary honey-bee differentiator versus generic pest spraying.
John Mukasa 2026-03-09 ask: legal statute name must appear verbatim per HBR licensure framing.
John Mukasa 2026-03-09 ask: bee page must call out ASAP's nonprofit-relocation partnership for live honey bee colonies.
John Mukasa 2026-03-09 ask (v8.4): bee-specific FAQ accordion (5 items) on the bee page. The id anchors the section for lock + future deep-linking from ads.

Robert 2026-05-18 (v8.6): the More Reviews button must land on ASAP's verified Google Business Profile (CID-based Maps URL), not a generic '/place/Name/@coords/' search that resolves to a search result instead of the GBP listing. CID 7456357551456980082 = hex 0x677a4db004e50c72 from the canonical Maps data hash for ASAP Pest & Wildlife Removal.
Same — applies to the About-page More Reviews button.

Robert 2026-05-26 screenshot regression: the About page reference uses a cream divider/carat below the navy hero, not the orange-tooth CTA banner. This lock anchors the page-specific Webflow reference structure after the stale 'all inner pages' banner rule caused a false QA pass.
Robert 2026-05-26 About screenshot regression: the About desktop header contact pill must use the Webflow orange pill treatment, not the generic dark navy pill that passed broad shared-header checks.
Robert 2026-05-26 About screenshot regression: 'The peace we give' must be a separately positioned subtitle matching removeasap.com/about geometry, not a second H1 span inheriting the wrong rhythm.
Robert 2026-05-26 About screenshot regression: the Our Aim body must render as the large reference paragraph block, not the previous small stacked card-style text.
Robert 2026-05-26 About screenshot regression: reference About copy uses paragraph breaks inside the body copy; collapsing to separate small paragraphs changed visual height and rhythm.
Robert 2026-05-26 About screenshot regression: mobile About header uses the same two-line Webflow menu glyph as the reference, not the generic three-line hamburger.

Robert 2026-05-26 About visual QA regression: About typography must load Adobe Fonts immediately; a delayed loader leaves fallback metrics that can falsely pass structure while rendering visibly wrong.
Robert 2026-05-26 Pest Control screenshot regression: the page needs its own Webflow reference hero, not the generic orange inner-page shell that falsely passed structural checks.
Robert 2026-05-26 Pest Control screenshot regression: reference uses a cream phone band directly under the pest-control hero title, not the generic orange CTA banner.
Robert 2026-05-26 Pest Control screenshot regression: the Don't let pests section needs the Webflow navy underline/arrow treatment.
Robert 2026-05-26 Pest Control screenshot regression: the Why Choose section uses the reference navy block, not the prior cream card grid.
Robert 2026-05-26 Pest Control screenshot regression: the Got Bugs CTA must use the reference dark branded CTA treatment.
Robert 2026-05-26 Pest Control screenshot regression: mobile Pest Control header uses the same two-line Webflow menu glyph as the reference, not the generic three-line hamburger.

Robert 2026-05-26 Pest Control visual QA regression: Pest Control typography must load Adobe Fonts immediately; fallback Arial changes the hero width and can falsely pass structure while rendering wrong.
Robert 2026-05-26 Commercial screenshot regression: Commercial needs its own Webflow reference hero, not the generic navy/hollow inner-page shell that falsely passed older shared locks.
Robert 2026-05-26 Commercial screenshot regression: the reference uses a filled navy Commercial hero title on cream, not an outlined H1 inherited from other pages.
Robert 2026-05-26 Commercial screenshot regression: the full-width process artwork must sit directly under the cream hero/intro like removeasap.com/commercial-services.
Robert 2026-05-26 Commercial screenshot regression: partner logos belong on the navy reference band, not a cream grid with mixed colored logos.
Robert 2026-05-26 Commercial screenshot regression: the business type grid needs the Webflow rounded outlined card treatment.
Claude 2026-05-26 Commercial QA note: reference copy uses a plain hyphen-minus here; lock it so typography cleanup cannot silently drift from the source page.
Robert 2026-05-26 Commercial screenshot regression: mobile Commercial header uses the same two-line Webflow menu glyph as the reference, not the generic three-line hamburger.
Robert 2026-05-26 Commercial visual QA regression: Commercial typography must load Adobe Fonts immediately; fallback metrics were one cause of false page-level passes.
Robert 2026-05-26 Services screenshot regression: Services needs its own Webflow reference hero/intro, not the generic cream Services shell that falsely passed shared inner-page locks.
Robert 2026-05-26 Services screenshot regression: the reference uses an orange-outlined Services hero title on navy.
Robert 2026-05-26 Services screenshot regression: the orange phone band belongs inside the navy hero and must not be replaced by the generic CTA banner.
Robert 2026-05-26 Services screenshot regression: the full-width inspection/capture/exclude/warranty artwork must sit between the intro and Trapping section like removeasap.com/services.
Robert 2026-05-26 Services screenshot regression: the wildlife list CTA must use the reference navy/cream treatment rather than the previous cream generic block.
Robert 2026-05-26 Services full-page visual QA: the Services page footer/contact area must use the Webflow reference layout, not the compressed shared boxed-input footer.
Robert 2026-05-26 Services full-page visual QA: the Webflow contact form includes the Type other underline field below the issue selector.
Robert 2026-05-26 Services full-page visual QA: the reference footer centers the quoted privacy policy copy below the form.
Robert 2026-05-26 Services screenshot regression: mobile Services header uses the same two-line Webflow menu glyph as the reference, not the generic three-line hamburger.
Robert 2026-05-26 Services visual QA regression: Services typography must load Adobe Fonts immediately; fallback metrics change the hero and service-heading widths.

Robert 2026-05-26 homepage screenshot regression: the homepage needs page-specific Webflow reference overrides for hero rhythm, CTA bar sizing, mobile animals/backgrounds, and homepage nav color. Broad shared styling checks are insufficient.

Robert 2026-05-26 homepage screenshot regression: the hero CTA copy must use the Webflow-style sentence-case service text plus CALL NOW label, not the all-caps letter-spaced block that drifted from removeasap.com.

required_strings.string: aria-current="page" class="text-navy font-bold text-sm uppercase tracking-[0.15em] hover:text-orange transition-colors">Home
required_strings.rationale: Robert 2026-05-26 homepage screenshot regression: homepage desktop nav must mark Home current so only Home renders navy while the inactive links render burnt orange like removeasap.com.

Gemini 2026-05-26 homepage QA block: inactive desktop homepage nav links must be explicitly locked to the Webflow burnt-orange state rather than inheriting Tailwind navy or a muted browser-rendered variant.
Gemini 2026-05-26 homepage QA block: the Home nav item must be explicitly locked to the active navy state on the homepage.
Gemini 2026-05-26 homepage QA block: the We Remove & Exclude eyebrow needs the reference-style orange underline and tooth accent, not a plain text label.
Robert 2026-05-26 homepage visual QA: desktop nav spacing must match the Webflow 30px rhythm; a 22px gap right-aligned the menu too far to the right.
Robert 2026-05-26 homepage visual QA: We Remove & Exclude eyebrow typography must use the Webflow 15px subheading size, not Tailwind text-sm.
Robert 2026-05-26 homepage visual QA: We Remove underline must match the Webflow underline width under the full subheading text.
Gemini 2026-05-26 homepage QA block: the We Remove section needs the grunge texture plus pale leaf field seen in the reference, not the plain/leaf-only cream background.
Gemini 2026-05-26 homepage QA block: tablet/mobile We Remove content must keep the Webflow side inset instead of collapsing to a 16px Tailwind gutter.
Gemini 2026-05-26 homepage QA block: mobile We Remove labels must match the reference 35px Webflow tab text, not the oversized 48px Tailwind mobile default.
Gemini 2026-05-26 homepage QA block: the mobile ampersand must overlap from the left of the More label like the reference, not consume normal flex space before the word.
Gemini 2026-05-26 homepage QA block: mobile header uses the reference two-line menu glyph, not the generic three-line hamburger.

Robert 2026-05-26 homepage visual QA regression: the homepage hero must not wait 5 seconds for Adobe Fonts; fallback Arial changes the hero width and creates false visual mismatches against removeasap.com.

Robert 2026-05-18 (v8.6): wildlife card overlays (Armadillo, Bat, etc.) previously used 'opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100' with no mobile fallback — hover doesn't fire on touch so the animal descriptions were completely invisible on mobile. The new pattern always shows the overlay on mobile (opacity-100) and preserves hover-reveal on md+ (md:opacity-0 md:group-hover:opacity-100). Burn incident: bob 2026-05-18 marked 'optimize for Mobile View' addressed via v8.2 (closeout_ref bbf3100) when v8.2 didn't actually touch this overlay pattern.
Same — applies sitewide on the wildlife listing page.

required_strings.string: nmsdc-2024.png" alt="NMSDC Certified 2024" class="max-h-12 max-w-full object-contain invert opacity-70"
required_strings.rationale: Robert 2026-05-18 (v8.6): the NMSDC certified-2024 PNG is rendered in light gray on transparent background (authored for dark-bg context). Without the 'invert' filter (and 70% opacity to match neighboring monochrome logo weight), it renders gray-on-cream and is invisible on ASAP's cream partner band. v8.5 fixed five other partner SVGs by swapping fill to navy; PNG can't be edited the same way, so the CSS filter is load-bearing. Removing 'invert' regresses to invisible.

script_src_contains.rationale: Allaine 2026-05-18: scroll-state JS must remain in main.js (else nav stays transparent forever). The .is-scrolled class is toggled at runtime and never appears in HTML, so this lock fetches main.js itself and greps the body. Guards both the CSS class hook and the toggle handler.

stylesheet_href_contains.rationale: Robert 2026-05-26 About screenshot regression: About's orange Webflow contact pill styling lives in external CSS, so HTML-only locks are insufficient.
Robert 2026-05-26 About visual QA: the desktop contact pill and intro copy must use Webflow-sized text, not Tailwind's smaller local defaults.
Robert 2026-05-26 About visual QA: the desktop contact pill must preserve the Webflow letter-spaced treatment.
Robert 2026-05-26 About visual QA: the Our Aim body copy must keep the reference line length at desktop while capping at the wide Webflow width.
Gemini 2026-05-26 About QA block: the About hero must use the Webflow SVG as a section background at its native opacity so the dot/spore texture remains visible near the hero headline.
Gemini 2026-05-26 About QA block: the About hero background must preserve the Webflow 120vw sizing, not a dimmed object-cover crop that shifts or hides the dot texture.
Gemini 2026-05-26 About QA block: the stale dimmed full-cover image layer must stay disabled so it cannot suppress the Webflow hero background texture.
Robert 2026-05-26 About visual QA: the mobile cream divider must begin at the Webflow reference height, not ride high under the subtitle.
Gemini 2026-05-26 About QA block: mobile About hero title must clear the logo/tagline; the earlier 50.6875px offset caused a rendered overlap.
Gemini 2026-05-26 About QA block: after lowering the mobile About title, the subtitle gap must tighten so The peace we give keeps the reference vertical position.
Gemini 2026-05-26 About QA block: the Our Aim section must use the Webflow background SVG as a section background, not an object-cover layer that shifts the watermark on mobile.
Robert 2026-05-26 About visual QA: mobile divider height must shrink when the hero grows so the intro section stays at the reference vertical position.
Gemini 2026-05-26 About QA block: mobile Our Aim watermark must sit behind the paragraph area instead of drifting into the intro lockup.
Robert 2026-05-26 Pest Control screenshot regression: page-specific hero CSS must exist so the generic theme-orange first section cannot take over.
Robert 2026-05-26 Pest Control visual QA: the hero must preserve the Webflow contact-hero background sizing behind the title/art.
Robert 2026-05-26 Pest Control screenshot regression: the cream phone-band treatment must be locked in CSS, not inferred from HTML class presence.
Robert 2026-05-26 Pest Control screenshot regression: the left roach art is part of the reference hero composition.
Robert 2026-05-26 Pest Control screenshot regression: the right ant art is part of the reference hero composition.
Robert 2026-05-26 Pest Control mobile visual QA: mobile hero title must stay on one reference-sized line instead of wrapping into the oversized generic title.
Gemini 2026-05-26 Pest Control QA block: wide/desktop header contact pill must use the Webflow constrained width so it does not break the header at 1280px or 1982px.
Gemini 2026-05-26 Pest Control QA block: the navy header logo intentionally overflows upward like the Webflow reference; removing this makes the first viewport visibly wrong.
Gemini 2026-05-26 Pest Control QA block: mobile Pest Control title must keep the reference height and avoid crowding the hero.
Gemini 2026-05-26 Pest Control QA block: mobile Pest Control title width is tuned to the live Webflow screenshot; fallback structure checks alone missed this.
Robert 2026-05-26 Commercial screenshot regression: page-specific hero CSS must exist so the generic theme-navy first section cannot take over.
Robert 2026-05-26 Commercial screenshot regression: the process artwork must be locked as full-width section art, not a padded gallery thumbnail.
Robert 2026-05-26 Commercial screenshot regression: the partner section must keep the navy Webflow band.
Robert 2026-05-26 Commercial screenshot regression: the business grid card geometry must be locked in CSS.
Robert 2026-05-26 Commercial visual QA: mobile Commercial hero title must use the Webflow 34px filled-title size, not the oversized hollow inner-page default.
Robert 2026-05-26 Commercial visual QA: mobile partner-logo row must preserve the reference horizontal overflow rhythm rather than collapsing into a stacked grid.
Robert 2026-05-26 Services screenshot regression: Services needs page-specific navy hero CSS so the generic theme-navy first section cannot take over.

Robert 2026-05-26 Services screenshot regression: the fixed header must remain transparent over the navy reference page while scrolling; the generic cream scroll state made the Services page visibly wrong.
Robert 2026-05-26 Services visual QA: desktop Services hero must preserve the Webflow contact-hero background sizing.
Robert 2026-05-26 Services screenshot regression: the process artwork is a full-width section image, not a padded thumbnail or omitted asset.
Robert 2026-05-26 Services screenshot regression: service detail blocks must stay on the navy reference background.
Robert 2026-05-26 Services full-page visual QA: contact/footer styling must be page-specific and match the Webflow reference scale.
Robert 2026-05-26 Services full-page visual QA: the contact form fields use orange underline/top-line styling, not boxed gray borders.
Robert 2026-05-26 Services full-page visual QA: submit button must be the small orange rounded Webflow-style button, not a full-width boxed button.
Robert 2026-05-26 Services full-page visual QA: desktop Services body copy must use Webflow's 25px/37.5px paragraph rhythm, not the smaller compressed responsive fallback.
Robert 2026-05-26 Services full-page visual QA: contact section top/bottom padding must match Webflow's footer spacing.
Robert 2026-05-26 Services full-page visual QA: contact footer columns must match the Webflow two-column grid geometry.
Robert 2026-05-26 Services mobile visual QA: mobile Services hero title must match the Webflow 43.23px heading size.

Claude 2026-05-26 Services mobile visual QA block: mobile Services section/contact headings must match Webflow's 32px heading size so Reocurring Service and Dead Animal Removal do not expand the page vertically.
Claude 2026-05-26 Services mobile visual QA block: mobile Services headings must preserve the Webflow 32px/38.4px rhythm instead of the oversized 43px/51.6px fallback.
Robert 2026-05-26 Services mobile visual QA: mobile header logo must match the live Webflow logo geometry.

forbidden_strings.rationale: Robert 2026-05-18 (v8.5): the 'More Photos' button on About linked to ASAP's Google Maps business listing. ASAP is a mobile-service company with no retail location; sending visitors to a Maps page implies otherwise. Removed in v8.5. Lock guards against re-insertion.

Robert 2026-05-18 (v8.5): the 'Google Photos →' link on Wildlife had a text/destination mismatch (label said Google Photos, href pointed to Google Maps). Removed in v8.5. Lock guards both the broken label and re-insertion of any Google Photos CTA without a real photo album URL.

Robert 2026-05-26 screenshot regression: About must not render the orange CTA banner under the hero. The authoritative removeasap.com/about/ reference uses a navy hero, cream divider/carat, and navy intro section instead.
Robert 2026-05-26 Pest Control screenshot regression: Pest Control must not render the generic orange CTA banner under the hero; the authoritative reference uses a cream phone band inside the hero.
Robert 2026-05-26 Services screenshot regression: Services must not render the generic CTA banner as a separate cream-page break; the reference phone bar is part of the navy hero.
Robert 2026-05-26 Services full-page visual QA: the Webflow Services contact block lists hours, phone, and email only; the shared footer's Book online row is a visible mismatch.

Robert 2026-05-18 (v8.6): logo-orange-tagline.png has the 'Your pest and wildlife extraordinaires' cursive baked into the PNG in #F3EFDD (cream) — contrast ratio ~1.02:1 on the white/cream header. Tagline was invisible on desktop and mobile. logo-orange.png is the same logo with the tagline rendered in dark navy and is the canonical header asset. Lock guards against reverting to the broken asset.

Robert 2026-05-18 (v8.6): the original More Reviews href was a generic Google Maps search ('/place/ASAP+Wildlife+Removal/@33.734354,-84.242248,10z/') that doesn't anchor to ASAP's verified GBP — it resolves to a Maps search result, not the business listing. Replaced with a CID-based URL. Lock guards against reverting to the generic-search form.
Same — applies to the About-page button.

forbidden_substring_in_svg_fill.rationale: Robert 2026-05-18 (v8.5): partner logos tps.svg, ccm.svg, cw.svg, gfl.svg, ksm.svg were authored with cream (#F2EDDC) fills for a dark-bg context. On ASAP's cream partner band the logos rendered as cream-on-cream and were invisible to visitors. Fills swapped to navy (#212936). Lock prevents future regression where a partner SVG ships with cream fill against the cream partner section.

required_assets.rationale: Robert 2026-05-26 screenshot regression: the About hero must carry the large orange bird from the removeasap.com/about/ reference, overlapping the cream divider/carat area.
Robert 2026-05-26 Pest Control screenshot regression: the left roach hero art is present on removeasap.com/pest-control-services and must stay visible in our hero.
Robert 2026-05-26 Pest Control screenshot regression: the right ant hero art is present on removeasap.com/pest-control-services and must stay visible in our hero.

Robert 2026-05-26 Pest Control screenshot regression: the Webflow reference places the navy ASAP monster above the Pest Control title.
Robert 2026-05-26 Pest Control screenshot regression: the cream phone band uses the cream carat/tooth, not the generic orange CTA tooth.
Robert 2026-05-26 Pest Control screenshot regression: the first cream content block uses the Webflow navy underline arrow.

Robert 2026-05-26 Services screenshot regression: the reference places the gray ASAP monster above the Services hero title.
Robert 2026-05-26 Services screenshot regression: the live Webflow Services page includes the full-width inspection/capture/exclude/warranty artwork before Trapping.
Robert 2026-05-26 Services screenshot regression: the navy Services header uses the orange logo with cream tagline, matching removeasap.com/services.
Item #9: Contact-form section uses the canonical monster, not the thin-mouth-line SVG.
Item #9: Contact-form section uses the canonical monster, not the thin-mouth-line SVG.
Homepage CTA banner shows the orange-tooth element.

Required asset for the 'More' tab decorative '&'. Layout history: v8.1 over-removed both ampersands; v8.2 restored the after-'More' instance; v8.4 (Robert 2026-05-18) moved it BEFORE 'More' with negative margin-right per removeasap.com reference (underlaps the M from the left, larger, opacity-80). Current authoritative structure is anchored by the we-remove-amp class — see required_strings entry below.

forbidden_assets.rationale: Robert 2026-05-26 screenshot regression: the About page does not use the orange-tooth CTA banner in the authoritative Webflow reference, so arrow-orange.svg on /about/ is now a page-specific regression.

required_class_on_h1.rationale: Robert 2026-05-26 screenshot regression: About's hero title uses the hollow orange outlined treatment from the Webflow reference, not a filled cream H1.
Item #1: Inner-page H1s use the hollowed-out style matching homepage Peace-of-Mind treatment.

min_pattern_count.rationale: Item #4: Homepage partner-logo grid has 9 logos in a normalized grid (was unaligned flex-wrap before 2026-05-12).

Robert 2026-05-26 Commercial screenshot regression: live Webflow Commercial reference shows the dark partner band with TPS, KSM, C&W, GFL, CCM, and NMSDC overflow badge, not the broader homepage-style 9-logo cream grid.
